precision_regression_status: no regression observed; sample insufficient for phase exit
notes: The global tool is an editable installation of the reviewed worktree, avoiding stale committed-snapshot packaging during the canary.
Claude, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, and Pi produced their platform-native deny shapes through the live global binary.
Cursor exposes 17 installed event groups and OpenCode resolves the installed plugin through its effective debug configuration.
The pre-existing Slopgate auto-update service, timer, and dangling wants symlink were removed after ownership checks; timestamped backups were retained.
No post-rollout result/subprocess record contained an error, traceback, import failure, or module-load failure in the initial canary window.
The one non-synthetic blocking denial was SHELL-001 on a command containing both stderr suppression and `|| true`; it was labeled a true positive.
Three cross-platform validation replays exercised first-write shadowing, projected-lint shadowing, and retry-budget denial and are excluded from the live precision denominator.
The maximum evaluation includes collector-backed lint work and is not representative of the median hook path.
Two PY-CODE-012 advisory false positives were confirmed in ordinary tests: capsys output inspection and multi-member inspection of an object under test.
PY-CODE-012 now skips test-directory paths while preserving production findings; focused regression coverage, 2,265 project tests, basedpyright, Ruff, and slopgate lint all pass.
PY-IMPORT-003 now permits edits and patch updates to existing private implementation files while preserving post-write enforcement for newly introduced stacked private paths.

The global editable tool was refreshed after the detector module move; live `slopgate handle --platform opencode` replay suppressed PY-CODE-012 for capsys test inspection, retained it for production feature envy, and rejected malformed JSON.
Phase 4 remains open until the labeled live denial sample and canary duration are large enough to assess precision and sustained overhead.
